---
title: "Research"
---

## VSMC Phenotypic Switch Modulators

Vascular smooth muscle cells (VSMCs) are highly plastic cells that can transition between a quiescent **contractile phenotype** and a proliferative/migratory **synthetic phenotype**. This switch is central to the pathogenesis of atherosclerosis and in-stent restenosis (ISR).

My doctoral work employs a multi-stage bioinformatics pipeline integrated with experimental validation to:

1. **Discover gene signatures** of the VSMC phenotypic switch using bulk RNA-seq datasets (GSE193817, GSE69637, GSE253003)
2. **Identify candidate modulators** through transcription factor activity analysis and drug-gene interaction networks
3. **Validate key candidates** (Resveratrol, Galangin) via Western Blot, immunofluorescence, and qPCR in primary human aortic smooth muscle cells (HAoSMC)

**Key tools**: R (limma, edgeR, WGCNA, clusterProfiler), Python, Seurat, GSVA

---

## ABCB5⁺ Mesenchymal Stem Cells

ABCB5⁺ MSCs represent a rare population of skin-derived stem cells with potent immunomodulatory and regenerative properties. In collaboration with the Starkuviene lab, I contributed to a comprehensive review of their differentiation potential and clinical translation perspectives.

---

## Methods

| Category | Techniques |
|----------|-----------|
| Wet Lab | Western Blot, Immunofluorescence, RT-qPCR, Wound Healing Assay, Cell Viability (CTB) |
| Bioinformatics | Bulk RNA-seq analysis, scRNA-seq, Mendelian Randomization, WGCNA, GSVA, Gene Ontology/KEGG enrichment |
| Cell Models | Primary HAoSMC, HeLa cells, ABCB5⁺ MSCs |
